root_cause: Pod cannot be scheduled due to node resource fragmentation preventing effective preemption
evidence: Repeated FailedScheduling events within short time window
Aggregate cluster capacity exceeds pod request, but no node fits the full request (cpu=3000m, memory=10737418240 bytes)
Scheduler reports insufficient resources across multiple nodes

Dominant scheduler message: 0/3 nodes are available: 2 Insufficient cpu, 1 Insufficient memory. preemption: 0/3 nodes are available: 3 No preemption victims found for incoming pod, 3 Preemption is not helpful for scheduling.

causes.message: Cluster resources are fragmented across nodes
No single node has sufficient contiguous resources for the Pod
Scheduler cannot place Pod due to per-node bin-packing constraints

pod:fragmented-preemption-pod: Pod repeatedly failed scheduling due to fragmented node capacity
node:node1: Node cannot satisfy the pod's full resource shape on its own
node:node2: Node cannot satisfy the pod's full resource shape on its own
node:node3: Node cannot satisfy the pod's full resource shape on its own

likely_causes: Cluster resources are fragmented across nodes
Pod resource requests are too large for any single node shape
Existing workload placement leaves unusable resource gaps
Preemption cannot free a node with the required combined CPU and memory
suggested_checks: Check per-node allocatable versus requested resources
Evaluate pod CPU and memory requests
Inspect current pod distribution across nodes
Consider autoscaling or larger node shapes
